steps.title: Translate the pricing into an expression
steps.content:
The first person costs $\$9$. The remaining $n - 1$ people each cost $\$6$, contributing $6(n-1)$ dollars.

Total charge: $g(n) = 9 + 6(n - 1)$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice at $n = 5$
steps.content:
• A. $g(n) = 15n \Rightarrow g(5) = 75$. Wrong.
  • B. $g(n) = 15n - 9 \Rightarrow g(5) = 66$. Wrong.
  • C. $g(n) = 6n + 3 \Rightarrow g(5) = 33$. Correct, and it matches the structure $9 + 6(n-1)$.
  • D. $g(n) = 6n + 9 \Rightarrow g(5) = 39$. Wrong (treats all $n$ people as additional, then adds $\$9$).
That matches choice C — C is correct.
